name: Additive alpha-1 adrenergic antagonism
actions: Avoid unnecessary combinations of agents with significant alpha-1 blocking activity.
If combined, titrate slowly, counsel patients to rise slowly from sitting or lying positions, and monitor BP for orthostatic drops (educational).

rationale: Both drugs antagonize the alpha-1 adrenergic receptor.
Combined alpha-1 blockade produces additive vasodilation and loss of sympathetic vascular tone, increasing the risk of orthostatic hypotension, reflex tachycardia, dizziness, and falls.
Risk is compounded in elderly patients, those on antihypertensives, or patients with autonomic dysfunction.

explanation_template: {A_name} and {B_name} both antagonize the alpha-1 adrenergic receptor. Combined use additively reduces vascular sympathetic tone, increasing orthostatic hypotension and falls risk (educational).

references.citation: PD stacking: additive alpha-1 antagonism produces cumulative vasodilation and orthostatic hypotension risk.
